title: pastinaca sativa
intro: Pastinaca sativa, also known as wild parsnip or giant hogweed, is a biennial plant native to Europe and western Asia. It is known for its toxic sap, which can cause severe skin irritation and burns, but its roots and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Pastinaca sativa include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Pastinaca sativa can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, although caution is advised due to the plant's toxic sap, and its health benefits include supporting immune function and reducing inflammation.
